Package org.apache.juneau.marshall.stream

Interface ArrayRecordWritable

All Known Implementing Classes:
BsonSerializer, BsonSerializer.Base64, BsonSerializer.SpacedHex, BsonSerializerSession, CborSerializer, CborSerializer.Base64, CborSerializer.SpacedHex, CborSerializerSession, JcsSerializer, JcsSerializerSession, Json5lSerializer, Json5lSerializerSession, Json5Serializer, Json5Serializer.Readable, Json5SerializerSession, JsonlSerializer, JsonlSerializerSession, JsonSchemaSerializer, JsonSchemaSerializer.Readable, JsonSchemaSerializerSession, JsonSerializer, JsonSerializer.Readable, JsonSerializerSession, MsgPackSerializer, MsgPackSerializer.Base64, MsgPackSerializer.SpacedHex, MsgPackSerializerSession, ParquetSerializer, ParquetSerializerSession, YamlSerializer, YamlSerializer.Readable, YamlSerializerSession
public interface ArrayRecordWritable
Capability role for serializers that can emit each element of a top-level wire array as a separate record.

The symmetric writer-side counterpart of ArrayRecordReadable. Capability is detected via instanceof.

See Also:
  • ArrayRecordReadable — the symmetric reader role.
  • Serializers and Parsers

  • Method Summary

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    default boolean
    isArrayRecordStreaming()
    Streaming-quality signal queryable before opening a cursor.
    RecordWriter
    serializeArrayRecords(Object output)
    Opens a RecordWriter that wraps each write(...) call as one element of a top-level wire array.
    default RecordWriter
    serializeArrayRecords(Object output, int expectedCount)
    Variant of serializeArrayRecords(Object) for length-prefixed binary formats that require the element count up front (msgpack, bson).

  • Method Details

    • serializeArrayRecords

      RecordWriter serializeArrayRecords(Object output) throws IOException
      Opens a RecordWriter that wraps each write(...) call as one element of a top-level wire array.
      Parameters:
      output - The output.
      Returns:
      A new element-streamed RecordWriter.
      Throws:
      IOException - If a problem occurred opening the underlying output.

    • serializeArrayRecords

      default RecordWriter serializeArrayRecords(Object output, int expectedCount) throws IOException
      Variant of serializeArrayRecords(Object) for length-prefixed binary formats that require the element count up front (msgpack, bson). Other formats may ignore the count and behave the same as serializeArrayRecords(Object).
      Parameters:
      output - The output.
      expectedCount - The number of elements that will be written. Must match the number of RecordWriter.write(Object) calls before RecordWriter.close().
      Returns:
      A new element-streamed RecordWriter.
      Throws:
      IOException - If a problem occurred opening the underlying output.

    • isArrayRecordStreaming

      default boolean isArrayRecordStreaming()
      Streaming-quality signal queryable before opening a cursor.
      Returns:
      true for true element streaming (json/cbor/msgpack); false for the buffered RecordAdapter.arrayWriter(org.apache.juneau.marshall.serializer.SerializerSession, java.lang.Object)-backed formats (yaml, parquet, bson).
